title: ECOGEO 'Omics Training: 1.0 Unix and Bioinformatics
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"btv3r","text":"This protocol details the use of various unix commands commonly used in bioinformatics. Open this protocol inside the virtual machine (details in \"Start Instructions\") for easy copy, paste of commands into the command line terminal window. before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"5l25n","text":"Before starting, please visit the ECOGEO website for more information on this 'Introduction to Environmental 'Omics' training series. The site contains a pre-packaged virtual machine that can be downloaded and used to run all of the protocols in this protocols.io collection. In addition to the VM, the website contains video and presentations from our initial 'Intro to Env 'Omics' workshop held at the Univ. of Hawai'i at Manoa on 25-26 Jul 2016.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":34,"length":15}],"data":[]},{"key":"8ses4","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"mepm","text":"Please email ‘ecogeo-join@earthcube.org’ to join the ECOGEO listserv for future updates.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"E06C5078B0354A7BB3965A597C444CF7","url":"http://earthcube.org/group/ecogeo"}}}}
steps: Open terminal window a
Use ls to list items in the current directory. ls lists items in the current directory

Many commands have additional options that can be set by a '-' ls -als -lls -lt lists all files/directories, including hidden files '.'lists the long formatlists the long format, but ordered by date last modified ls -a ls -l ls -lt lists all files/directories, including hidden files '.' lists the long format lists the long format, but ordered by date last modified
cd - change directory cd ecogeo/
pwd (present working directory) can be used to show the current directory. pwd prints the path to the current directory cd /home/c-debi/ecogeo/unix
Move to the root directory. a This is where everything is stored in the computer. All the commands we are running live in /bin. cd /

Change directory to home Change directory to c-debi Change directory to ecogeo Change directory to unix List contents Change directory to data Change directory to root Tabs can be used to auto complete names.
Change directory to unix/data in one step $ cd /home/c-debi/ecogeo/unix/data

cd '..' allows you to step back up through the path directory. Display present working directory path. cd ..pwd moves back in the path directory cd .. pwd moves back in the path directory /home/c-debi/ecogeo/unix
List contents of BioinfPrograms
Make a directory named "storage". mkdir storage
The 'touch' command allows you to create a blank file of the input name. touch temp.txt creates a blank file of the input name
The 'cp' command allows you to copy a file and can be used to move a copy of a file to a directory. $ cp
The 'mv' or move command "destroys" the original and places the content elsewhere.
Using copy: $ cp temp.txt newtemp.txt$ cp temp.txt ../ $ cp temp.txt newtemp.txt $ cp temp.txt ../
List contents.
Utilize move command: $ mv newtemp.txt oldtemp.txt$ mv oldtemp.txt /home/c-debi/ecogeo/unix/data $ mv newtemp.txt oldtemp.txt $ mv oldtemp.txt /home/c-debi/ecogeo/unix/data
Remove oldtemp.txt $ rm oldtemp.txt
Remove storage directory: $ rm -r storage

Create a directory called bestdirectoryever Change directory to bestdirectoryever Create a file called glam.txt Change glam.txt to formerglam.txt Remove formerglam.txt Change directory to unix Remove bestdirectoryever
group12_contigs.fasta group20_contigs.fasta group24_contigs.fasta FASTA files - specific format > Header line, contains ID and information about... ATGATAGCTAGCAGCAGCTA[...] 80bp and then a newline.
'head' will allow you to view the first 10 lines of a file. $ head [filename] default displays the first 10 lines
'tail' allows you to view the last 10 lines of a file. $ tail [filename] default displays last 10 lines
'less' allows you to scroll through a file using arrow keys or spacebar = advanced page | b = reverse page | q = quit $ less [filename]

Use head to display the first 10 lines of group12_contigs.fasta Display the first 5 lines of group12_contigs.fasta Display the last 10 lines of group12_contigs.fasta Display the last 5 lines of group12_contigs.fasta $ head -10 [filename] $ head -5 [filename]
grep - file pattern searcher $ grep
wc - count the number of words, lines, characters
Use grep on group12_contigs.fasta All quotation marks should work for copy, paste into the VM. Also, you can use single '>' or double quotes ">" for any grep command
How many? Combine grep and wc? Use the "|" (pipe) symbol

Use the same technique to determine the number of sequences in group20_contigs.fasta . What about the number of matches to “47” in group12_contigs.fasta ? Or “_47”? Can also write as $ grep '>' group12_contigs.fasta | grep 47 $ grep

Redirecting output to file: ">" = retrieve > = write to file $ grep “>” group12_contigs.fasta > group12_ids$ grep “>” group12_contigs.fasta > group12_ids_with_47 $ grep “>” group12_contigs.fasta > group12_ids $ grep “>” group12_contigs.fasta > group12_ids_with_47
cat - has multiple functions: $ cat group12_ids_with_47 With a single input - prints file contents
With ‘>’ cat has the same function as cp $ cat group12_ids_with_47 > temp1_ids$ cp group12_ids_with_47 temp2_ids $ cat group12_ids_with_47 > temp1_ids $ cp group12_ids_with_47 temp2_ids
Double check to make sure temp1_ids = temp2_ids
Concatenate files with cat - most important function:
Check contents of duplicate_ids using less or cat
Grab all of the contigs IDs from group20_contigs.fasta that contain the number “51” $ grep
Concatenate the new IDs to the duplicate_ids file in a file called multiple_ids
uniq - can be used to remove duplicates or identify lines with 1 occurrence or multiple occurrences Compare multiple_ids before and after uniq $ uniq multiple_ids
Why was there no change? uniq has a weakness, can only identify duplicates in adjacent lines sort - sort lines in a file alphanumerically
Clear all present files with temp in title $ rm temp* ‘*’ - acts as a wildcard, so any file that starts with temp would be identified and removed, no matter the suffix

How do temp1_ids & temp2_ids compare? -d = identify duplicates (temp1_ids) -u = identify unique (temp2_ids) $ sort multiple_ids | uniq -d > temp1_ids$ sort multiple_ids | uniq -u > temp2_ids $ sort multiple_ids | uniq -d > temp1_ids $ sort multiple_ids | uniq -u > temp2_ids
temp1_ids = group12_ids_with_47 & temp2_ids = group20_ids_with_51

sed - modify files a file based on the issued commands Want a list of sequence IDs without the ‘>’? sed ‘s/C/c/’ between the single quotes, s ubstitute the occurrence of upper case C to lower case c $ sed ‘s/C/c/’ clean_ids$ sed ‘s/_/./’ clean_ids$ sed ‘s/>//’ clean_ids > newclean_ids $ sed ‘s/C/c/’ clean_ids $ sed ‘s/_/./’ clean_ids $ sed ‘s/>//’ clean_ids > newclean_ids

seqmagick Wrapper designed to utilize built in Biopython modules to manipulate and change FASTA files Requires Biopython : http://fhcrc.github.io/seqmagick/ Discussed in video: convert - produce a modified new file mogrify - change the input file info - present information of files in a directory Additionally: backtrans-align, extract-ids, quality-filter, and primer-trim $ seqmagick
Execute seqmagick convert: $ seqmagick convert --include-from-file newclean_ids group12_contigs.fasta newgroup12_contigs.fasta

How many sequences are in newgroup12_contigs.fasta ? $ seqmagick extract-ids newgroup12_contigs.fasta | wc$ seqmagick info *fasta $ seqmagick extract-ids newgroup12_contigs.fasta | wc $ seqmagick info *fasta

Store the information generated by ‘seqmagick info’ in a new file fasta_info $ cut$ cut -f 2 fasta_info$ cut -f 2,4 fasta_info$ cut -f 2-4 fasta_info cut - pulling out columns from a table file-d allows for the assignment of the type of delimiter between fields, if not TAB-f delineates which fields to preserve, starting at 1 $ cut $ cut -f 2 fasta_info $ cut -f 2,4 fasta_info $ cut -f 2-4 fasta_info cut - pulling out columns from a table file -d allows for the assignment of the type of delimiter between fields, if not TAB -f delineates which fields to preserve, starting at 1
history - prints a sequential list of all commands in the current session echo $PATH - lists the directories for which the OS is checking for commands and data

nano - in window text editor $ nano fasta_info Additional text can be entered like any text editorTo close out - Ctrl+X, hit ‘Y’, then ENTERCreate a new file - nano and then enter file name after Ctrl+X $ nano fasta_info Additional text can be entered like any text editor To close out - Ctrl+X, hit ‘Y’, then ENTER Create a new file - nano and then enter file name after Ctrl+X
Simple bash scripts: Text file with a list of commands that can be executed as a batch. Look at the contents of simplebashscript
Simple bash scripts: Text file with a list of commands that can be executed as a batch. Look at the contents of simplebashscript chmod - change file modes $ chmod 775 simplebashscript
Plain text file -> executable text file. $ ./simplebashscript
creator: Elisha M Wood-Charlson
